module.title: Week 23: Detection Engineering & Sigma/YARA
module.focus: Detection lifecycle, Sigma rules, YARA rules

topics.title: Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand the detection engineering lifecycle
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Walk through the detection engineering lifecycle for a specific use case
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the detection engineering lifecycle?
topics.interviewAnswer: Threat research (understand TTPs via MITRE ATT&CK), Rule development (write detection logic), Testing (validate against known attacks and benign activity), Deployment (push to production), and Tuning (adjust thresholds and exceptions).

quiz.question: What is step 1 of detection engineering?
What is an atomic detection?
options: Single event that indicates malicious activity

quiz.question: What is a behavioral detection?
options: Sequence of events indicating attack pattern

quiz.question: What rule caught SUNBURST activity?
options: Unusual DLL loads from temp dirs by svchost.exe

quiz.question: What framework maps detection use cases?
Which best describes Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Detection Engineering Fundamentals can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: Sigma Rules
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: Sigma Rules is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Write portable Sigma detection rules
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Write a Sigma rule for brute force detection with proper metadata
topics.interviewQuestion: What is a Sigma rule structure?
topics.interviewAnswer: Title, id, status, description, references, tags (MITRE mapping), author, date, logsource (product, service, category), detection (selection keywords and condition), falsepositives, and level (informational to critical). The condition combines selections with boolean logic.

quiz.question: What does Sigma do?
What does the logsource field specify?
options: Which log source the rule applies to

quiz.question: What combines Sigma selections?
options: The condition field with boolean logic

quiz.question: How many SIEM formats can Sigma convert to?
What does sigma-cli do?
options: Converts Sigma to target SIEM formats

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 2: Sigma Rules?

topics.title: Day 3: YARA Rules
topics.description: Write YARA rules for malware detection
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: YARA Rules is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Write YARA rules for malware detection
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Write YARA rules for ransomware, MinGW-compiled files, and TrickBot detection
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the components of a YARA rule?
topics.interviewAnswer: Meta (metadata), strings (text, hex, or regex patterns to match), and condition (logic combining strings with AND/OR/NOT, counting occurrences, checking file size, entry point, and PE headers). YARA modules extend with PE, ELF, Math (entropy), and Hash analysis.

quiz.question: What are the three YARA rule sections?
What is a hex string in YARA?
What does the PE module check?
What does the Math module provide?
What did Stuxnet YARA rules detect?
Which best describes Day 3: YARA Rules?
quiz.explanation: Day 3: YARA Rules is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 3: YARA Rules typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

topics.title: Day 4: Custom Detection Logic & Behavioral Rules
topics.description: Build behavioral detection rules from baselines
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: Custom Detection Logic & Behavioral Rules is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Build behavioral detection rules from baselines
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Design a custom behavioral detection rule with baseline definition
topics.interviewQuestion: How do behavioral detection rules work?
topics.interviewAnswer: They model 'normal' activity and alert on deviations: a database server initiating outbound connections (potential exfiltration), a user logging in at 3 AM (account compromise), or a server quadrupling outbound traffic (data theft). Baselines are built over 30-90 days with statistical thresholds.

quiz.question: What do behavioral rules model?
options: Normal activity and alert on deviations

quiz.question: What is a common baseline period?
What threshold is commonly used?
What did the bank's custom rule detect?
What is a deviation in behavioral detection?
Which best describes Day 4: Custom Detection Logic & Behavioral Rules?
quiz.explanation: Day 4: Custom Detection Logic & Behavioral Rules is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 5: Detection Rule Testing & Deployment Project
topics.description: Create a rule testing and deployment pipeline
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Detection Rule Testing & Deployment Project is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Create a rule testing
- Deployment pipeline
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Design a deployment pipeline with unit tests, staging, and production KPIs
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the rule testing pipeline?
topics.interviewAnswer: Unit test against known malicious samples (Atomic Red Team), benign test against 7-30 days of historical logs for FP baseline, staging deployment monitoring alerts without incidents, tuning, then production deployment with continued FP monitoring for 2 weeks.

quiz.question: What is a unit test for detection rules?
What is a benign test?
options: Run against 7-30 days of historical logs

quiz.question: What is the staging phase?
options: Deploy to subset monitoring alerts without incidents

quiz.question: What FP rate is acceptable for production?
What tool runs adversary technique simulations?
Which best describes Day 5: Detection Rule Testing & Deployment Project?
quiz.explanation: Day 5: Detection Rule Testing & Deployment Project is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.
